text.title: PMax API Gotchas and Agent Rules
text.summary: Google Ads API-specific failure modes for Performance Max campaigns, including enums, customer_id format, and multi-account considerations.

rule.statement: customer_id must be provided as an unhyphenated 10-digit string (e.g., "1234567890"); the hyphenated form from the UI ("123-456-7890") causes authentication errors.
rule.rationale: The Google Ads API does not accept hyphenated customer IDs. Agents that copy-paste from the UI clipboard will silently fail with confusing auth-related error messages.

rule.statement: MCC accounts require the login-customer-id header set to the MCC account ID in the API client; omitting it causes 401 errors on all child account calls.
rule.rationale: Multi-account agents accessing child accounts must authenticate through the MCC. Without the login-customer-id header, calls fail at the authentication layer before reaching the campaign logic.

rule.statement: Pin the Google Ads API version and refresh client stubs when bumping versions; asset field type enums change across versions (e.g., MARKETING_IMAGE vs LANDSCAPE_LOGO naming).
rule.rationale: PMax asset type enums are updated as Google adds new asset types. An agent using enum values from a deprecated version will receive INVALID_ARGUMENT errors that do not name the changed field.

rule.statement: PMax budget amount_micros uses Google Ads conventions: $50/day = 50,000,000 micros (multiply by 1,000,000). LinkedIn costs are decimal floats — do not mix unit systems across platforms.
rule.rationale: Agents working across Google and LinkedIn in the same session commonly apply the wrong unit convention. $50 submitted as 50 to Google Ads creates a $0.000050/day budget that generates zero impressions.

rule.statement: Before creating a PMax campaign, validate that at least one conversion action with include_in_conversions_metric=True exists at the account level and has received conversions in the last 30 days.
rule.rationale: PMax silently optimizes for the best available conversion action. If the account has no active conversion actions, or only micro-conversions, the campaign will optimize for meaningless events.

list.item: Enabling the campaign: human brand-safety review of generated copy required
Changing target ROAS or target CPA: human approval required
Toggling URL expansion: human approval required
Any asset swap touching more than 50% of current assets: human review required
